name: Eclipse
title: The Great Witch
investmentNote: Strong PvP attacker and solid PvE dark DPS, but outclassed in endgame PvE by Blade and Luvencia. Nightmare Bunny is the key costume — without it, investment is questionable. Not recommended for heavy new investment as she's slowly losing meta relevance to newer units like Venaka.
lore: Eclipse is the owner of the Pagan Tower — a great witch of the eastern Svern Continent and a succubus who has lived far longer than any human. When Ratel's party first visited the tower on Olstein's instructions, they found Eclipse locked in a desperate defense against Cocytus' relentless assault. Despite her enigmatic and slightly haughty exterior, she ultimately joined the cause after uncovering a ring and sealed relic left behind by her former companion Charon. She was ranked first in the 1.5th Anniversary character popularity vote, earning the free Dream Bride costume as a reward from the community — a testament to her enduring fan favourite status.
overview: Eclipse is a Magic Dark DPS who specializes in draining enemy SP while dealing respectable damage. Her impact on the PvP meta was massive when Nightmare Bunny released — it instantly became the most popular PvP costume in the game, changing defensive formations overnight and forcing players to gear 105-110% MRES just to counter her. Two years later, she's been somewhat power-crept by DJ Venaka (higher M.ATK, 50% MRES shred vs Eclipse's 20%) and newer 1000%+ multiplier costumes, but Nightmare Bunny remains one of the best value 3 SP PvP costumes in the game. In PvE, her base M.ATK is low (308 vs average of 352), which limits her damage ceiling — Blade and Luvencia sit a tier above her for endgame content. However, her SP battery utility via Nightmare Bunny is invaluable in Tower of Salvation and long fights where SP regeneration is scarce.
keyInsight: Eclipse currently has four costumes — The Dimension Witch, Nightmare Bunny, Beach Vacation, and Dream Bride — giving her a complete rotation cycle with no downtime. Her core power comes entirely from Nightmare Bunny's unique pre-hit MRES shred mechanic: she reduces enemy MRES by 15-20% BEFORE the first hit lands, which is a property unique to her among all characters. Without Nightmare Bunny, investing in Eclipse is questionable.

synergies.name: Helena (B-Rank Idol)
synergies.note: M.ATK + Crit Rate buff gets Eclipse to 100% crit

synergies.name: Refithea (PWB)
synergies.note: Property + chain bonus for dark advantage fights

synergies.name: Diana
synergies.note: Elemental DMG aura — boosts Eclipse's dark damage on advantage targets

synergies.name: Celia
synergies.note: Chain stacking before Eclipse's nuke turn

synergies.name: Luvencia
synergies.note: Complementary dark DPS — Luvencia carries, Eclipse supports with SP battery

costumes.name: The Dimension Witch
costumes.description: Eclipse's base costume, recruitable from the Story Pack 6 — Pagan Tower pub. Terra Drain heals 35% Max HP, attacks 3 times at 120% Magic ATK each (360% total), and decreases 3 SP for each enemy hit. The skip-shaped AoE pattern is awkward to position — you'll often wish it were 'very front' instead. SP drain is irrelevant in PvE (making this effectively a vanilla dark attack), but in PvP it forces the enemy team down to only 6 SP on their following turn. Solid baseline damage but unspectacular without Nightmare Bunny.

skills.name: Terra Drain
skills.desc: Heal HP based off 35% Max HP. Attack 3 times, each dealing Magic DMG at 120% M.ATK. Decrease 3 SP for each attacked target.

tips: Recruitable from Story Pack 6 pub — get a free copy early.
SP drain only matters in PvP. In PvE this is just a damage skill.
Skip-shaped AoE is hard to position — consider enemy layouts carefully.
Skill Potential 3 for the SP reduction is the main upgrade target.

costumes.name: Nightmare Bunny
costumes.description: Eclipse's signature limited costume and the reason she defined the PvP meta for over a year. Dream Eater decreases Magic Resist by 15% (20% with potentials) for 4 turns BEFORE dealing damage — a pre-hit shred mechanic unique to Eclipse. Then attacks 3 times at 75% M.ATK each (up to 3×220% at +5 = 660% after shred), acquires 1 SP per unique hit, and drains 1 SP per unique hit from enemies. The long cross AoE (5 long, 5 wide) is nearly impossible to dodge in PvP. At only 3 SP, she needs to hit just one enemy to pay for herself via SP gain. At +0 her damage multipliers are low — battery utility is good immediately but damage needs dupes. She has been somewhat power-crept by DJ Venaka (50% MRES shred, higher M.ATK) but remains one of the best value 3 SP PvP costumes.

skills.name: Dream Eater
skills.desc: Decrease Magic Resist by 15% for 4 turns (pre-hit). Attack 3 times at 75% M.ATK each. Acquire 1 SP per unique hit. Decrease 1 SP per unique hit from enemies.

tips: The MRES shred applies BEFORE the first hit — unique mechanic in the entire game.
In Tower of Salvation (no SP regen), Eclipse can be swapped in/out repeatedly to refill team SP.
Forces enemies to build 90+ MRES to counter her in PvP — hugely warping effect on gear meta.
At +0, damage is low but SP utility is already excellent. Invest dupes for damage.
Skill Potential 1 for damage upgrades is the priority here.
No longer recommended for heavy NEW investment — better to pick up on rerun if available.

costumes.name: Beach Vacation
costumes.description: Eclipse's highest damage costume — her 'ultimate' skill. Summer Climax attacks 2 times at up to 450% M.ATK each (900% total before chains at +5) with reliable 3×3 AoE coverage. The 11-turn cooldown means you can typically only use it once per fight, which makes it a nuke-turn save — fire it after Nightmare Bunny's MRES shred for maximum damage. Excellent for Fiend Hunts and Guild Raids as the big damage turn. At +0-2, Dream Bride (free +5) actually outdamages it — you need at least +3 Beach Vacation to surpass the free alternative.

skills.name: Summer Climax
skills.desc: Attack 2 times, each dealing Magic DMG at 200% M.ATK (up to 450% at +5).

tips: Save for nuke turn — 11-turn CD means one use per fight in most content.
Needs +3 minimum to outdamage the free Dream Bride +5.
Best used after Nightmare Bunny's MRES shred for maximum output.
Not worth pulling if you don't have Nightmare Bunny.

costumes.name: Dream Bride
costumes.description: Eclipse's free limited costume, obtainable at +5 through the 2nd Anniversary event missions — awarded after she won the 1.5th Anniversary popularity poll. The skill Honeymoon Getaway deals Magic DMG in a heart-shaped AoE with excellent tile coverage. With a 5-turn cooldown (much more usable than Beach Vacation's 11 turns) and the unique pattern, this fills the gap in Eclipse's rotation nicely. Since it's free at +5, it provides immediate value to all players and serves as Eclipse's second-strongest damage option after Beach Vacation +5. Skill Potential 3 for SP cost reduction is the key upgrade.

skills.name: Honeymoon Getaway
skills.desc: Deal Magic DMG equal to 350% of M.ATK with wide tile coverage (heart-shaped AoE, up to 625% total at +5).

tips: Free at +5 from the 2nd Anniversary event — no investment needed.
Outdamages Beach Vacation at +0-+2, so use this until Beach Vacation is +3+.
5-turn cooldown is much more rotation-friendly than Beach Vacation's 11 turns.
Good bond potential for both PvP and general PvE due to offensive stats.
Skill Potential 3 for SP reduction is the main upgrade target.

weapons.name: Star Caller Ornament
weapons.description: Eclipse's exclusive accessory. Gives M.ATK% and Crit DMG. Despite sounding good, in PvE a Venomous Touch (Crit DMG / Crit DMG) typically gives more damage output than the exclusive. In PvP, the exclusive is good for tankier Eclipse builds where you trade some damage for survivability. Not a must-pull.

weapons.name: Venomous Touch
weapons.description: Best-in-slot for Eclipse in PvE damage builds. Double Crit DMG stacking outperforms her exclusive gear's M.ATK% bonus when paired with Helena's Crit Rate buff to reach 100% crit.

gear.stat: Crit Rate % (to reach 50% — Helena brings to 100%)

teams.name: PvP Magic Opener
teams.description: Eclipse's primary use case. Nightmare Bunny opens with 3 SP — MRES shred lands before damage, SP drain disrupts the enemy, and SP gain refills your own bar. Follow up with Beach Vacation or Dream Bride on subsequent turns for lethal damage on MRES-shredded targets. Helena provides the M.ATK buff and Crit Rate to push Eclipse to 100% crit. Granhildr can taunt Nightmare Bunny away, so position carefully.

rotation.action: Nightmare Bunny → MRES shred + SP drain + damage (3 SP)
Dimension Witch or Honeymoon Getaway → Follow-up damage + SP drain
Beach Vacation or Dream Bride → Nuke on MRES-shredded targets

teams.name: Tower of Salvation SP Battery
teams.description: In Tower of Salvation's higher floors, SP regeneration is zero — you get no SP regen at all. Nightmare Bunny Eclipse is a lifesaver here: she generates 1 SP per unique hit and only costs 3 SP, meaning she can be swapped in and out repeatedly to keep the team's SP bar full. She can even be rotated through Crystal Battle Lv10 as a dedicated SP refill tool. Her damage is secondary — the SP battery function is the primary value.

rotation.action: Swap Eclipse in → Nightmare Bunny → Generate SP → Swap out
Cycle Eclipse in whenever SP runs low

teams.name: Dark FH / Guild Raid
teams.description: When dark has elemental advantage, Eclipse serves as a safe-pick DPS on team 2 or 3. Open with Nightmare Bunny for MRES shred and SP battery, then save Beach Vacation for the nuke turn. She is outclassed by Luvencia and Blade for team 1 damage, but fills the dark DPS slot reliably when you need multiple teams. Her various AoE shapes can sometimes be awkward for hitting boss weak tiles — check the layout before committing.

rotation.action: Nightmare Bunny → MRES shred + SP battery
Dimension Witch or Dream Bride → Sustain damage
Beach Vacation → Maximum burst on shredded target

tips.title: Nightmare Bunny or Bust
tips.body: Eclipse as a DPS requires an invested Nightmare Bunny, which is a limited costume. Her base Dimension Witch is serviceable but unremarkable, and Beach Vacation's 11-turn cooldown is prohibitive without Nightmare Bunny's rotation support. If you don't have Nightmare Bunny, investing in Eclipse is questionable.

tips.title: Unique Pre-Hit MRES Shred
tips.body: Nightmare Bunny decreases Magic Resist BEFORE the first hit lands — a property unique to Eclipse in the entire game. Normally, shreds apply after the first hit. This makes her opening burst significantly scarier and is the reason she's still competitive despite power creep.

tips.title: Low Base M.ATK
tips.body: Eclipse's base M.ATK is only 308 — below the 352 average and far below Venaka (363) or Scheherazade (385). This inherently caps her damage ceiling in endgame PvE, making Blade and Luvencia strictly better for dark advantage Fiend Hunts.

tips.title: Tower of Salvation MVP
tips.body: In Tower of Salvation's highest floors (no SP regen), Nightmare Bunny Eclipse is a lifesaver. She generates 1 SP per hit at only 3 SP cost — swap her in/out repeatedly to keep your team's SP bar full. This utility alone justifies owning her at +0.

tips.title: Free Dream Bride at +5
tips.body: Dream Bride Eclipse is obtainable at +5 for free from the 2nd Anniversary event. It deals 625% in a heart-shaped AoE with a 5-turn CD. This means every player already has a solid Eclipse damage costume — only pull Beach Vacation if you can get it to +3+ to outdamage the freebie.

tips.title: Fan Favourite Hall of Famer
tips.body: Eclipse won the 1.5th Anniversary popularity poll and entered the Hall of Fame alongside Dalvi and Morpeah in the 2nd Anniversary vote. Her cultural impact on the BD2 community is enormous — the Dream Bride costume was a direct community reward.
